rulesGroups.heading: General
rulesGroups.description: General conduct, officiating responsibilities, and score reporting rules.

rules.html: Everyone should encourage fair play and good sportsmanship.
Be respectful to other players, referees and officials.
Please vacate the sports hall promptly at the end of the session.
Please make sure to accomplish your officiating duties assigning at least: the principal referee, the scorer and two line judges.
The first team acting as a referee for the day should bring a copy of the scoresheet provided in the captains's whatsapp group.
The officiating team is responsible to write down the score in the scoresheet provided.
The winner team is responsible to submit the game score using the app once the game is finished.
The last team refereeing should take a picture of the scoresheet and send it to the captains group to get a record of the scores as a backup.

rulesGroups.heading: Teams & Players
rulesGroups.description: Rules about team composition, eligibility, and borrowing players.

rules.html: Each team must have 6 players on court all times.
Each team is required to have at least 2 women on court at all times, regardless of their playing position.
A team will forfeit their game should either they not have enough (female) players or they not have enough players on court (6 players), though they could still play for fun.
Teams can borrow a player from another team on game day under exceptional circumstances such as injuries or illnesses.
A player cannot play for more than one team, in case not following the rules, the teams where the player was playing will get 2 points deducted for each day.

rulesGroups.heading: Games
rulesGroups.description: Format and timing rules for matches and daily tiebreaks.

rules.html: 6v6 timed games - please stick to the starting and ending times on the schedule.
Normal volleyball rules apply.
Teams can use different lineup for each set.
No limit of regular substitutions (A → B, B →A).
Irregular substitutions (A → B, C →B) are not allowed.

Games will consist of:
04:00 min of warm-up
33:00 min of game (2 sets of 16:00 min each)
01:00 min to swap courts
When the time is up, finish playing the point if a rally is in progress.
If the scores are level at the end of the game time, play ONE more point to decide the winner.

The tiebreaker in case teams having the same amount of points at the end of the day will be the best coefficient:
Coefficient = Points Scored / Points Against

rulesGroups.heading: Finals
rulesGroups.description: Qualification and tiebreak rules for finals week.

rules.html: The 4 teams with the best overall of Total Points at the end of the 4th week will play the Division 1 final.
The teams within the 5th to the 8th best overall of Total Points at the end of the 4th week will play the Division 2 final.
The teams within the 9th to the 12th best overall of Total Points at the end of the 4th week will play the Division 3 final.
The teams within the 13th to the 16th best overall of Total Points at the end of the 4th week will play the Division 4 final.
The tiebreaker for teams having the same amount of points at the end of the 4th week would be the times each team played Division 1, Division 2, Division 3 and Division 4.

If the tie persists after applying the previous criteria, then, the best coefficient will be applied:
Coefficient = Points Scored / Points Against

rulesGroups.heading: Scoring - Division 1
rulesGroups.description: Points, demotion, and bonus rules for Division 1.

rules.html: Wining teams will get 3 points.
Losing team will get 2 points if their final points score within 10 of the wining team, otherwise will get 0 points.
The team getting the lowest score on the day will get demoted to Division 2.
The teams in Division 1 will get 1 bonus point per week after 1st week.

rulesGroups.heading: Scoring - Division 2
rulesGroups.description: Points, promotion, and demotion rules for Division 2.

rules.html: Wining teams will get 2 points.
Losing team will get 1 point if their final points score within 12 of the wining team, otherwise will get 0 points.
The team getting the best score on the day will get promoted to Division 1.
The team getting the lowest score on the day will get demoted to Division 3.

rulesGroups.heading: Scoring - Division 3
rulesGroups.description: Points, promotion, and demotion rules for Division 3.

rules.html: Wining teams will get 2 points.
Losing team will get 1 point if their final points score within 14 of the wining team, otherwise will get 0 points.
The team getting the best score on the day will get promoted to Division 2.
The team getting the lowest score on the day will get demoted to Division 4.

rulesGroups.heading: Scoring - Division 4
rulesGroups.description: Points and promotion rules for Division 4.

rules.html: Wining teams will get 2 points.
Losing team will get 1 point if their final points score within 16 of the wining team, otherwise will get 0 points.
The team getting the best score on the day will get promoted to Division 3.
